relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Rome voyage begins under custody.
Aristarchus joins the sea-crossing.
Kindness lets Paul receive care from friends.
Contrary winds shape the route.
The ship crosses toward Myra.
An Alexandrian ship carries them toward Italy.
Slow sailing and difficult winds bring them near Crete.
Fair Havens is reached with difficulty.
The dangerous season has arrived.
Paul perceives hardship and loss ahead.
Technical confidence outweighs prophetic caution.
The majority chooses a better winter harbor.
A gentle wind makes the plan look wise.
The violent Northeaster overturns appearances.
The ship yields to the storm.
The skiff is barely secured.
The ship is undergirded while fear steers action.
Cargo is thrown into the sea under pressure.
Even ship’s tackle is surrendered.
Without sun or stars, hope of saving/healing fades.
Paul stands after long fasting and names the ignored warning.
Courage is offered: lives will not be lost.
A messenger stands by Paul in the night.
Rome remains necessary, and all aboard are graciously given.
Trust in the Divine word becomes courage.
The ship must come to an island.
Fourteen nights in the storm bring midnight suspicion of land.
Soundings show the bottom rising.
Anchors fall, and they pray for day.
Sailors try to flee under pretense.
Saving/healing requires remaining together in the ship.
The skiff is cut away.
Paul urges food after fourteen days.
Food becomes part of saving/healing, with no hair lost.
Before all, Paul gives thanks, breaks bread, and eats.
Encouragement spreads, and all take food.
Two hundred seventy-six lives are held in the ship.
Fed, they lighten the vessel.
Day reveals an unknown land and a possible beach.
Anchors are abandoned and the ship turns toward shore.
The vessel runs aground and begins to break.
Soldiers plan prisoner death to prevent escape.
The centurion protects Paul and blocks the killing.
On planks and fragments, all are brought safely to land.
